title: Ensure security contact information is registered

input_row.title: Ensure security contact information is registered
input_row.description: AWS provides customers with the option of specifying the contact information for account's security team. It is recommended that this information be provided.
input_row.rationale: Specifying security-specific contact information will help ensure that security advisories sent by AWS reach the team in your organization that is best equipped to respond to them.
input_row.audit: Perform the following to determine if security contact information is present: From Console: 1. Click on your account name at the top right corner of the console 2. From the drop-down menu Click My Account 3. Scroll down to the Alternate Contacts section 4. Ensure contact information is specified in the Security section From Command Line: 1. Run the following command: aws account get-alternate-contact --alternate-contact-type SECURITY 2. Ensure proper contact information is specified for the Security contact.
input_row.remediation: Perform the following to establish security contact information: From Console: 1. Click on your account name at the top right corner of the console. 2. From the drop-down menu Click My Account 3. Scroll down to the Alternate Contacts section 4. Enter contact information in the Security section Internal Only - General From Command Line: Run the following command with the following input parameters: --email-address, --name, and --phone-number. aws account put-alternate-contact --alternate-contact-type SECURITY Note: Consider specifying an internal email distribution list to ensure emails are regularly monitored by more than one individual.

step1_initial_assessment:
**AUDIT APPROACH:** Automated

**APPROACH DECISION:**
This control can be fully verified via AWS APIs, as the `aws account get-alternate-contact` and `aws account put-alternate-contact` commands are specifically designed for checking and modifying the security contact information, respectively. Both the AWS CLI and boto3 SDK support these operations, confirming that this control can be audited without requiring any manual intervention or human judgment.

**PROGRAM NAME:** aws_account_security_contact_information_registered

**AUTOMATION DETAILS:**
- **AWS CLI Command:** Use the `aws account get-alternate-contact --alternate-contact-type SECURITY` command to check if security contact information is specified.
- **Boto3 SDK Method:** Use `boto3.client('account').get_alternate_contact(AlternateContactType='SECURITY')` to retrieve the security contact information.
- **Specific Properties to Validate:** The response should include `name`, `emailAddress`, and `phoneNumber` for the Security contact type. 
- **Pass/Fail Criteria:** The configuration is compliant (Pass) if all three properties (`name`, `emailAddress`, `phoneNumber`) are present and filled out. If any of these fields are missing or empty, the control fails.
- **Example Check:** Use the specified commands/methods to verify that valid security contact information is registered, which helps the organization respond to security advisories effectively.
